title: BLKANDNBLK warning reported for a non-assignment use
body:
The attached example ([error-BLKANDNBLK.tar.gz](https://github.com/verilator/verilator/files/7695690/error-BLKANDNBLK.tar.gz)) gives this error:
```
%Error-BLKANDNBLK: mod.v:6:16: Unsupported: Blocked and non-blocking assignments to same variable: 'mod.fr'
    6 |   reg [31 : 0] fr;
      |                ^~
                   mod.v:62:21: ... Location of blocking assignment
   62 |    b__h333 = $fgetc(fr);
      |                     ^~
                   mod.v:39:16: ... Location of nonblocking assignment
   39 |     if (fr$EN) fr <= fr$D_IN;
      |                ^~
                   ... For error description see https://verilator.org/warn/BLKANDNBLK?v=4.215
```
claiming that `fr` is assigned in two different ways, but the location that it points to for the nonblocking assignment is not an assignment to `fr` (though `fr` is used in the RHS).  Am I missing some reason that this is being treated as an assignment?

This happens with a recent HEAD and an earlier release:
```
Verilator 4.215 devel rev v4.214-50-ga14394db
Verilator 4.200 2021-03-12 rev v4.110-43-g5022e81af (mod)
```
